notes: Lesson 17 Part 2 (act 100), 'Variable-size window', 128.23s, 6 short cues. Concrete variable-size walkthrough — longest run with no repeated character over the string a b c a b c b, with L/R boundary pointers. R expands greedily admitting a,b,c (window length 3); when the duplicate 'a' arrives at index 3 the rule breaks; L shrinks from the left, releasing the leftmost 'a' and advancing to index 1 so the window b,c,a is legal again. Right advances once per index and left advances at most once, so total travel is 2n = O(n). The skeleton is unchanged across variants — only the validity test swaps (a set for no-repeat, a count map for at-most-k-distinct); record the best length whenever the window is legal. Uses the arrays.py idiom lib (value_row, window_bracket, pointer, value_badge, complexity). Two review iterations. Round-1 (author): smoke-rendered every cue with manim -s. Round-2: after the first -ql render exposed a pointer() helper bug (it rendered ABOVE the row so the long-gap L pointer collided with the headline), fixed the helper to render BELOW the row pointing up, re-rendered, then READ start/mid/end for all 6 cues; all pass. cue05_end re-extracted @127.8s (near-EOF). Per-cue checklist (label legibility, highlighted element matches the beat, pointer clarity, contrast, nothing off-frame/overlapping via theme.guard, chunk timing within pinning tolerance): all pass. Final -ql 128.201s vs audio 128.23s (delta 0.029s, within tolerance).
